pageTitle.value: The Rolex Awards - Milestones

pageCaption.value: The Rolex Awards identify and invest in exceptional people around the world who are carrying out pioneering projects. Find out more about the Awards now.

WaPageTitle.value: The Rolex Awards - Milestones - Rolex.org

metadescription.value: Laureates of the Rolex Awards are continuing to make the world a better place. Discover what new directions their projects are taking on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: The Rolex Awards - The next chapter

text.value:
As the 21st century unfolds, Laureates of the Rolex Awards for Enterprise — as part of Rolex’s Perpetual Planet initiative — are continuing to make the world a better place with their inspiring work that advances knowledge, improve lives and protect the environment.

Discover what exciting new directions their projects are taking that will benefit life on the planet for generations to come.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Securing Nigeria's food supply

pageCaption.value: In 2010, Nigerian social entrepreneur Nnaemeka Ikegwuonu won a Rolex Award for Enterprise for creating a radio station to advise smallholder farmers. Now, his latest project, a network of solar-powered, walk-in cold rooms, is revolutionizing Nigeria’s food supply chain.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Securing Nigeria's food supply

metadescription.value: Social entrepreneur Nnaemeka Ikegwuonu's ColdHubs are revolutionizing Nigeria’s food supply chain by reducing post-harvest food loss. More on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Grégoire Courtine - The future of paralysis treatment - Rolex.org

pageCaption.value: Neuroscientist Grégoire Courtine is developing groundbreaking bioengineering technologies to treat spinal cord injury.

WaPageTitle.value: Grégoire Courtine - The future of paralysis treatment - Rolex.org

metadescription.value: Neurologist Grégoire Courtine is developing groundbreaking bioengineering technologies to restore mobility to paralysed people. More on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Protecting rainforests and species in India

pageCaption.value: Romulus Whitaker’s childhood fascination with snakes has led him to devote his life to protecting the environment, in particular rainforests.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Protecting rainforests and species in India

metadescription.value: Since winning a Rolex Award in 2008, conservationist Romulus Whitaker has founded three rainforest research stations across India. More on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: A quest to save India’s rainforests

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Luis Jácome and Andean condor conservation

pageCaption.value: Two centuries ago, the Andean condor soared across the skies of the Andes, but today its population has been massively reduced mainly by human interventions. For over three decades, Argentine biologist Luis Jácome has been working to save one of the world’s largest flying birds from extinction.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Saving the sacred condor

metadescription.value: Since winning a Rolex Award in 1996, Argentinian biologist Luis Jácome has released over 200 Andean condors back into the wild. More on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Rodrigo Medellín, a biologist defending bats

pageCaption.value: Mexican biologist Rodrigo Medellín has devoted his life to demonstrating how bats, one of the most universally feared and hated mammals, are, in fact, a boon to humanity and agriculture.

WaPageTitle.value: The Biologist Championing Bats

metadescription.value: Rolex Awards Laureate Rodrigo Medellín has devoted his life to researching, teaching and campaigning to protect bats in Mexico. More on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Rehabilitating dying lakes and ponds in India

pageCaption.value: In the face of rampant urbanization and the severe consequences of climate change, Indian environmentalist Arun Krishnamurthy has mobilized the public, including scores of young volunteers, to help clean, restore and rehabilitate his country’s lakes.

WaPageTitle.value: Reviving India’s blighted lakes

metadescription.value: Rolex Awards Laureate and environmentalist Arun Krishnamurthy has made it his life's purpose to revive dying waterbodies in India. More on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Piyush Tewari - Saving lives on India's roads - Rolex.org

pageCaption.value: Following a distress call informing him that his cousin had died in a road accident where no bystanders came forward to support the victim, Piyush Tewari was propelled into finding solutions to this problem in India and has since dedicated himself to saving thousands of lives across the country and beyond.

WaPageTitle.value: Piyush Tewari - Saving lives on India's roads - Rolex.org

metadescription.value: Rolex Awards Laureate Piyush Tewari has drastically reduced the number of road victims in India with first aid training programs. More on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Saving Brazil's forests to protect bird life

pageCaption.value: Anita Studer, a Swiss ornithologist who went to Brazil to study its rich array of bird life, has, through sheer determination and dedication, inspired Brazilians to save their forests and changed thousands of lives for the better in what is now her second home.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - How a bird saved a forest

metadescription.value: By dedicating her life to saving Brazilian forests, Swiss ornithologist Anita Studer has changed thousands of lives for the better. More on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: How a bird saved a forest

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Adventures in the underworld

pageCaption.value: The hidden realm of caves has opened up a new world to scientists and explorers thanks to speleologists such as Francesco Sauro whose many expeditions have provided an archive of time for future generations.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Venturer in the underworld

metadescription.value: Rolex Awards Laureate and speleologist Francesco Sauro explores the world's deepest caves to uncover previously unknown life forms. More on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Saving Africa's turtles and tortoises

pageCaption.value: Intrigued by turtles and tortoises as a young man, Tomas Diagne has devoted his life to studying and conserving these endangered species in his native Senegal and across Africa.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Africa’s ‘Father of Turtles’

metadescription.value: Once a youthful whim, Tomas Diagne's protection of  endangered turtles and tortoises is now inspiring change across Africa. Learn more on rolex.org

pageTitle.value: The call of the cloud forest - Rolex Awards

pageCaption.value: Sri Lankan conservationist Rohan Pethiyagoda has devoted more than two decades to a campaign to engage his fellow citizens in the regeneration of his island nation’s unique highland ecosystem, once defined by rich tropical forests shrouded by mist.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex Awards -  The call of the cloud forest

metadescription.value: Since the Rolex Award in 2000, Rohan Pethiyagoda's efforts to revive Sri Lanka's unique tropical ecosystem have found new momentum. More on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: The call of the cloud forest

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Rwanda's uplifting regal bird

pageCaption.value: Using education and persuasion, Olivier Nsengimana has liberated the much sought-after grey crowned crane from the gardens of Rwanda’s wealthy citizens and is now using a strategic wildlife management plan to ensure these magnificent birds flourish in their original habitats.

WaPageTitle.value: Rwanda’s uplifting regal bird - Rolex Awards

metadescription.value: Olivier Nsengimana has liberated the grey crowned crane from private gardens' captivity in Rwanda and suppressed the illicit trade. More on rolex.org

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Eye test brings equality

pageCaption.value: A billion people across the globe suffer from readily treatable eyesight problems. At least a third of them live in places where modern optical treatment never reaches, but that is rapidly changing, thanks to the inspired vision of British ophthalmologist Andrew Bastawrous.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Eye test brings equality

metadescription.value: Andrew Bastawrous has deployed an easy-to-use mobile phone-based technology to test the eyesight of people across Africa and Asia. More on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Saving the Snow Leopard

pageCaption.value: In Pakistan’s mountains, Shafqat Hussain is helping farmers maintain their livelihoods while learning to coexist with the big cats who prey on their herds.

WaPageTitle.value: Saving the snow leopard - Rolex Awards

metadescription.value: Environmental anthropologist Shafqat Hussain is helping farmers and the endangered snow leopard to coexist in harmony. Learn more on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Listening to the Earth

pageCaption.value: Since designing an underwater system to prevent ships from colliding with whales, bioacoustics pioneer Michel André has expanded his project to create a network of microphones that monitor the planet's myriad sounds and warn of threats to nature.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Listening to the Earth

metadescription.value: Michel André’s bioacoustics work has led to a growing network of underwater microphones that aims to protect marine life. Learn more on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - A vanished forest reborn

pageCaption.value: Working with dedicated farming families, Laury Cullen Jr. is restoring Brazil’s Atlantic Forest while contributing to the local economy and helping to fight climate change.

WaPageTitle.value: A vanishing forest reborn - Rolex Awards

metadescription.value: Laury Cullen Jr. has set up community tree nurseries and employed local people to restore 60,000 hectares of forest in Brazil. More on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Taking a volcano’s pulse

pageCaption.value: Eight hundred million people live in the shadow of fiery death, within striking range of one of Earth’s 500 historically active volcanoes. For Scottish volcanologist Andrew McGonigle, providing timely warning of an impending eruption is a goal that is both humanitarian and scientific.

WaPageTitle.value: Taking a volcano’s pulse - Rolex Awards

metadescription.value: Using drone technology to predict volcanic eruptions, Andrew McGonigle’s work is both humanitarian and scientific. Learn more on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Exploring submarine forests in Patagonia’s fjords

pageCaption.value: Discovering new species, never described to science, is just one aspect of the case the intrepid marine biologist Vreni Häussermann puts for the protection of the wild waters of the Patagonian fjords.

WaPageTitle.value: Exploring submarine forests in Patagonia’s fjords - Rolex Awards

metadescription.value: Rolex Award winner and biologist Vreni Häussermann is working to document Patagonia's fjords and protect its marine life. Learn more on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Exploring submarine forests in Patagonia’s fjords

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Stars of the deep

pageCaption.value: Little was known about the endangered whale shark, the world’s biggest fish, until Australian marine scientist Brad Norman found a way to identify individual animals using an algorithm developed by NASA.

metadescription.value: Little was known about the whale shark until Brad Norman started to study this species to conserve it. Read more about his research on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Mark Kendall - Medical alerts from a microwearable sticker - Rolex.org

pageCaption.value: From creating a pain-free vaccine patch, Australian scientist Mark Kendall is now developing microwearable devices that send early warnings when patients are experiencing events like heart attacks.

WaPageTitle.value: Mark Kendall - Medical alerts from a microwearable sticker - Rolex.org

metadescription.value: Scientist and 2012 Rolex Laureate Mark Kendall has produced microwearable sensors that provide critical medical information. Learn more on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Medical alerts from a sticker on your hand

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - How Greenland’s ice impacts climate change

pageCaption.value: A strange new life is blossoming within the icy carapace of Greenland, daubing thousands of square kilometres of pristine white landscape in an eerie patina of greens, reds, browns, purple and black.

WaPageTitle.value: Darkening hues of Greenland - Rolex Awards

metadescription.value: 2016 Rolex Young Laureate Joseph Cook is carrying out research on the function of microscopic plant life in the melting of the planet’s ice sheets.

title.value: The mighty Zambezi River and its secrets
The 2019 Rolex National Geographic Explorers of the Year - The Okavango Wilderness Project Team
Fernando Trujillo: For the love of river dolphins
